explicit_text: I was drafted into the United States Army in 1953, around May. I went through basic training at Fort Leonard Wood and then was deployed to Korea during the Korean War. My roles included working in supply and as a company clerk, handling weapons and clothing distribution. Serving in the Army was a big change from home, but I was proud to serve my country.

explicit_text: I served in the Korean War starting in 1953 after being drafted. We were stationed near the 38th parallel, building roads and patrolling the area. The conditions were harsh and cold, and I even suffered frostbite on my feet. Despite the hardships, I was proud to serve during this conflict.

explicit_text: I remember firing the M1 Garand rifle during training and patrols. The rifle had a strong kick, and the first time I fired it, I wasn't holding it right and got a cut under my eye. We also had to take the M1s apart and reassemble them quickly as part of our training at Inchon.
